title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Stomach Discomfort
intro_desc: If you're experiencing stomach discomfort, herbal essential oils might be just what you need to feel better. These oils are derived from plants that have been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. Herbal teas, in particular, are a great way to reap the benefits of these oils. They work by helping to calm the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation, and promoting the digestion process.  For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a popular choice for stomach discomfort. It has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and reduce pain. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is another herb that's commonly used to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. It's also been known to help with heartburn and acid reflux. Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, is a natural carminative, which means it can help relieve gas and bloating.  Drinking herbal teas made from these plants can have a positive impact on your life. Not only can they help alleviate stomach discomfort, but they can also promote healthy digestion, reduce stress, and improve your overall well-being. Plus, they're often caffeine-free and can be enjoyed hot or iced, making them a convenient and soothing addition to your daily routine. By incorporating herbal teas into your diet, you can take a natural approach to managing stomach discomfort and feel more comfortable in your own skin.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the digestive tract. The oils' warming effect stimulates blood flow and relaxes muscles in the stomach, easing cramps and spasms. The antioxidant properties of gingerol and shogaol also protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach muscles, Zingiber officinale essential oils helps to alleviate stomach discomfort and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Zingiber officinale roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Wash the roots with water, then dry them with a clean towel.
Grind 1/4 cup of the roots into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the root paste with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container. Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, easing discomfort and pain. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, blocks the action of an enzyme that breaks down stomach mucus, allowing the stomach to heal faster. The anti-inflammatory properties of flavonoids also help to calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of stomach discomfort. By reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can provide relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. Grind them into small pieces.
Use a 1-cup measuring cup to measure 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil). Pour it into a glass jar.
Add 1/4 cup of ground Glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the carrier oil. Stir well to combine.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids and store the Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a high concentration of active constituents like anethole, limonene, and pinene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. Anethole, in particular, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, while limonene has antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate harmful bacteria that can cause stomach discomfort. The antispasmodic properties of these constituents can also help to reduce cramping and spasms in the stomach, providing relief from symptoms of indigestion and nausea. By reducing inflammation and eliminating harmful bacteria, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to promote a healthy digestive system and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds, 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar.
Place the seeds in a saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into the glass jar, then discard the seeds.
Add 2 tablespoons of the simmered liquid to the carrier oil in the jar. Stir well.
Store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for skin application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains a high concentration of menthol and menthone, two active constituents responsible for its digestive benefits. Menthol helps to relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Menthone, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The essential oil's analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties also help to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea. By using Mentha x piperita essential oils, you can experience relief from stomach discomfort and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves (peppermint) and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat and add the peppermint leaves. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a still or a distillation apparatus. Collect the steam and condense it into a separate container.
Separate the essential oil from the condensed water using a separatory funnel or by letting it sit in the refrigerator for several hours, allowing the oil to solidify and separate from the water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains silymarin, a complex mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the stomach. Silymarin also has antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to stomach problems. Additionally, silymarin has a protective effect on the liver, which plays a crucial role in digestion and metabolism, and can help alleviate stomach discomfort caused by liver dysfunction. By reducing inflammation, neutralizing free radicals, and protecting the liver, Silybum marianum essential oils may provide relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the Silybum marianum flowers and carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well to coat the flowers.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will help the essential oil extract from the flowers.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a carrier oil or water for stomach discomfort relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils contains boiactive constituents like quinine, cinchonine, and cinchonidine. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach. Quinine, in particular, has been shown to have a direct effect on the stomach muscles, relaxing them and reducing spasms. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of Cinchona officinalis essential oils make it effective in soothing stomach discomfort and calming digestive issues. By using this oil, you can reduce stomach cramps, nausea, and other symptoms associated with stomach upset.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinchona officinalis leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Chop the Cinchona officinalis leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the chopped leaves, making sure they are completely covered
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard the leaves
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place, using 5-7 drops per serving for stomach discomfort

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains cinnamaldehyde, linalool, and camphor as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the stomach muscles. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to have a direct effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles that can cause stomach discomfort. The antispasmodic properties of the essential oil also help to reduce cramping and spasms in the stomach, providing relief from symptoms of indigestion and nausea. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach muscles, Cinnamomum verum essential oils can help to alleviate stomach discomfort and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum bark (Ceylon cinnamon), a carrier oil (coconut oil or jojoba oil), and a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Dry the Ceylon cinnamon bark in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours, or until it is completely dry.
Combine 2 cups of dried Ceylon cinnamon bark with 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat (100°F - 150°F) for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has been infused.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Store the Cinnamomum verum essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a carrier oil (coconut oil or jojoba oil) to help alleviate stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum essential oils contains compounds like limonene, pinene, and cymene, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the stomach. The essential oils also contain sesquiterpenes, which have a soothing effect on the digestive system and can help calm nausea. Additionally, cumin essential oil has a carminative effect, which means it helps release trapped gas and relieve bloating. This combination of properties makes Cuminum cyminum essential oils a potential aid in alleviating stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Cuminum cyminum herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of Cuminum cyminum seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil, like coconut or olive oil.
Heat 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add 1 cup of Cuminum cyminum seeds to the heated oil and let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the seeds.
Store the Cuminum cyminum essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops to relieve stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a powerful compound called curcumin, along with other bioactive constituents like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate discomfort. The essential oils' antioxidant properties can also neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to stomach irritation. Curcuma longa essential oils have been found to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing the risk of ulcers and other stomach problems. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, curcuma longa essential oils may help to provide relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa roots and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Grind the Curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1/4 cup of the ground powder with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass bottle.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bottle daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The bilobalide and ginkgolides in Ginkgo biloba essential oils also have a protective effect on the gut, supporting the health of the stomach lining and promoting digestion. By reducing inflammation and supporting gut health, Ginkgo biloba essential oils can help alleviate stomach discomfort and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil)
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl
Discard the solids and transfer the oil to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid
Label the bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for skin application

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains a high concentration of triterpenoids, including aloemodin, barbaloin, and aloe-emodin. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm stomach discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe-emodin and aloemodin can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, while barbaloin has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to protect and heal the stomach lining. The gel-like substance in aloe barbadensis essential oils also contains mucilages, which can form a protective barrier on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. This combination of bioactive compounds and soothing properties makes aloe barbadensis essential oils a potential natural remedy for stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for stomach discomfort
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, a clean glass jar, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Extract the gel from the Aloe leaves by scooping it out with a spoon and placing it in the glass jar.
Add 1/2 cup of water to the Aloe gel in the jar and stir well.
Cover the jar with the cheesecloth or coffee filter and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease stomach discomfort. Ginger tea, for example, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can calm an upset stomach. Peppermint tea can also help relax the muscles in your digestive system, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. It can be a soothing and calming way to ease stomach issues.
faq_safe: You can safely drink herbal teas for stomach discomfort daily, but be cautious with amounts and types. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are gentle and suitable for long-term use. Others, like ginger and licorice, can be too strong if consumed regularly. It's also essential to check the tea's ingredients and quality to avoid any potential harm.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with stomach discomfort, but how long it takes to feel relief varies. Some people notice a difference after one to two cups a day for a few days, while others may take a week or two to feel the effects. It's also individual, so it's hard to predict exactly when you'll start to feel better.
faq_time: If you're feeling stomach discomfort, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows you to digest the tea and its potential benefits without interfering with your evening meal.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for stomach discomfort. Certain herbs like peppermint and ginger can slow down the absorption of medications, while others like chamomile and licorice root can increase the risk of bleeding when taken with anticoagulants. This interaction can be unpredictable and may cause adverse effects.
faq_pregnant: When drinking herbal teas for stomach discomfort during pregnancy, be cautious. Some herbs can be too strong and affect the baby. Peppermint and chamomile teas are often okay, but others like raspberry leaf and ginger may be too stimulating. If you're unsure, it's best to check the label and choose a tea that's specifically labeled as safe for pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a gentle way to soothe stomach discomfort while breastfeeding. However, some herbs may pass into breast milk and affect your baby. Look for teas that are labeled as "safe for nursing mothers." Peppermint, chamomile, and ginger are often considered gentle options.
